results.question:
What things are important to consider when installing a fix pack for IIB running on a multi-instance environment? I am planning to apply a fix pack to my current version of IBM Integration Bus (IIB) using multi-instance brokers and I am looking for any important information regarding the process.

For example:

 

- installation and verification tasks
 

- is it possible to back out this fix pack in case there are issues
 

- any specific tasks for multi instance configuration
results.ground_truth:
When installing a fix pack for IBM Integration Bus (IIB) in a multi-instance environment, it is important to consider the following:

1. **Installation and Verification Tasks**: 
   - Ensure that the fix pack is compatible with your current version of IIB.
   - Follow the installation instructions provided by IBM for applying the fix pack to a multi-instance environment.
   - Verify that the installation is successful on all instances of IIB in your environment.

2. **Rollback Procedure**: 
   - Before applying the fix pack, make sure to have a backup of your current configuration and data.
   - Understand the rollback procedure in case the fix pack causes any issues.
   - Test the rollback procedure in a non-production environment to ensure it works as expected.

3. **Multi-Instance Configuration Tasks**:
   - Ensure that all instances of IIB are stopped before applying the fix pack.
   - Apply the fix pack to each instance of IIB individually to avoid any issues related to multi-instance configuration.
   - After applying the fix pack to all instances, start them one by one to ensure proper functioning.

4. **Testing After Installation**:
   - Once the fix pack is applied to all instances, perform thorough testing of your applications to ensure that they are functioning correctly.
   - Test the communication and interaction between different instances of IIB to ensure that the multi-instance setup is still working as expected.

5. **Verify Post-Installation Configurations**:
   - Double-check any custom configurations or settings that were applied to the multi-instance setup to ensure they are still in place after the fix pack installation.
   - Verify that any integration points or dependencies with other systems are not affected by the fix pack installation.

By considering these aspects, you can ensure a smooth installation process of the fix pack for IBM Integration Bus in a multi-instance environment and minimize any potential issues that may arise.

results.answer: The Event Dashboard issue you are experiencing with the Japanese filter in WebGUI 8.1 Fix Pack 11 is known and is related to the handling of Non-English locales. To work around this problem, you can temporarily change your browser's language setting to English, access the dashboard, and then switch back to Japanese. This should allow the dashboard to function correctly with the filter applied.

Title: TECHQA | Q20 passage 1

Health Dashboard; Event viewer; gauge; web gui TECHNOTE (TROUBLESHOOTING)

PROBLEM(ABSTRACT)
 The event list in the Event Viewer is not updated when clicking on gauges in the Netcool Health Dashboard when using Internet Explorer 9 or 10. When using IE11 or Firefox the problem is not seen. 

SYMPTOM
Step to reproduce: 

1. Log into DASH and open the Health Dashboard
2. Ensure that the event list shown is the Event Viewer, not the AEL
3. Ensure that the active view is the Self Monitoring view
5. Click on a selection of gauges.

Expected result: The event list is updated to show the events which match the related filter (if any such events exist).

Actual result: No update from the Event Viewer. The same events are showing.


CAUSE
This third party API:

var xmlDoc = new ActiveXObject( "Microsoft.XMLDOM" );

in /ibm/console/webtop/scripts/ajax.js causes an exception with some IE9/IE10 browsers.
Stepping over the above line throws the following exception:
eventTransformer: Exception caught while attempting to read payload. Error was:Automation server can't create object

The issue is caused by configuration changes on some Windows machines and is not a defect in OMNIbus Web GUI.


RESOLVING THE PROBLEM
Go to: 

" IE9/10 > Internet Options > Security > Custom Level" and set
1. "Run ActiveX controls and plug-ins" to Enable
2. "Script ActiveX controls marked safe for scripting" to Enable

SUBSCRIBE
You can track all active APARs for this component.



APAR STATUS
 * CLOSED AS PROGRAM ERROR.
    
   
   

ERROR DESCRIPTION
 *  An application server JVM can hang if a thread logging an FFDC
   hangs.
   
   The problem can be detected by observing the WSVR0605W hung
   thread notifications in the SystemOut.log.
   
   One thread will be running the FFDCFilter.processException()
   which can be invoked from any WebSphere Application Server
   component.  The thread will get stuck doing its work.
   In the following example, the thread which invoked
   FFDCFilter.processException() is stuck waiting on a response to
   the OracleConnectionWrapper.getMetaData() method call:
   
   [05/24/12 12:06:35:286 EST] 00000016 ThreadMonitor W
   WSVR0605W: Thread "ORB.thread.pool : 32" (000000d6) has
   been active for 304721 milliseconds and may be hung.  There
   is/are 16 thread(s) in total in the server that may be hung.
    at
   oracle.jdbc.OracleConnectionWrapper.getMetaData(OracleConnection
   Wrapper.java:150)
    at
   com.ibm.ws.rsadapter.spi.WSRdbManagedConnectionImpl.introspectSe
   lf(WSRdbManagedConnectionImpl.java:1480)
    at sun.reflect.NativeMethodAccessorImpl.invoke0(Native Method)
    at
   sun.reflect.NativeMethodAccessorImpl.invoke(NativeMethodAccessor
   Impl.java:60)
    at
   sun.reflect.DelegatingMethodAccessorImpl.invoke(DelegatingMethod
   AccessorImpl.java:37)
    at java.lang.reflect.Method.invoke(Method.java:611)
    at
   com.ibm.ws.ffdc.impl.Formatters$IntrospectSelfFormatter$1.run(Fo
   rmatters.java:99)
    at
   com.ibm.ws.ffdc.impl.Formatters$IntrospectSelfFormatter$1.run(Fo
   rmatters.java:96)
   
   ...
   
    at
   com.ibm.ffdc.util.provider.IncidentEntry.log(IncidentEntry.java:
   96)
    at com.ibm.ffdc.util.provider.Ffdc.log(Ffdc.java:90)
    at
   com.ibm.ws.ffdc.FFDCFilter.processException(FFDCFilter.java:114)
    at
   com.ibm.ejs.j2c.FreePool.createOrWaitForConnection(FreePool.java
   :1671)
    at com.ibm.ejs.j2c.PoolManager.reserve(PoolManager.java:2496)
   
   
   Looking later in the SystemOut.log, there will be more hung
   thread notifications which show that the
   FFDCFilter.processException() method is stuck waiting on an
   Ffdc.<init> call to complete.  The stack trace of such a thread
   will look like this:
   
   [05/24/12 12:42:35:914 EST] 00000003 ThreadMonitor W
   WSVR0605W: Thread "ORB.thread.pool : 29" (000007f4) has been
   active for 224151 milliseconds and may be hung.  There is/are 23
   thread(s) in total in the server that may be hung.
    at com.ibm.ffdc.util.provider.Ffdc.<init>(Ffdc.java:64)
    at
   com.ibm.ffdc.util.provider.FfdcProvider.getFfdc(FfdcProvider.jav
   a:289)
    at com.ibm.ffdc.impl.Ffdc.getFfdc(Ffdc.java:188)
    at
   com.ibm.ws.ffdc.FFDCFilter.processException(FFDCFilter.java:112)
    at
   com.ibm.ws.rsadapter.jdbc.WSJdbcPreparedStatement.pmiExecuteUpda
   te(WSJdbcPreparedStatement.java:1179)
    at
   com.ibm.ws.rsadapter.jdbc.WSJdbcPreparedStatement.executeUpdate(
   WSJdbcPreparedStatement.java:816)
   
   
   It is possible that the first FFDC thread which got hung (in
   this example, "ORB.thread.pool : 32") will eventually complete.
   If it does complete, the threads will start moving and the hang
   will clear up.  However, if it never completes, then it is the
   same as if a true thread deadlock had occurred.
   
   
   This same type of analysis can be performed using a
   javacore/thread dump, however, javacores are not always
   available.
   
   
    
   
   

LOCAL FIX

PROBLEM SUMMARY
 *  ****************************************************************
   * USERS AFFECTED:  All users of IBM WebSphere Application      *
   *                  Server V7.0 and V8.0                        *
   ****************************************************************
   * PROBLEM DESCRIPTION: Syncrhonization between FFDC            *
   *                      incidents can cause a server hang       *
   *                      when an unrecoverable problem           *
   *                      occurs in a given incident.             *
   ****************************************************************
   * RECOMMENDATION:                                              *
   ****************************************************************
   Syncrhonization between FFDC incidents can cause a server hang
   when an unrecoverable problem occurs in a given incident.
   
   
    
   
   

PROBLEM CONCLUSION
 *  FFDC has been modified to ensure that incidents can continue
   to be logged from other components when a given component's
   incident-handling hangs a thread.  The impact to this is that
   the FFDC summary log may indicate that the incident file of a
   bad thread was created, while the thread may not have returned
   so the incident file will not exist.  Therefore, a missing
   incident entry that might have appeared in a summary file is
   an indication that the component logging to FFDC did not
   return control.
   
   The fix for this APAR is currently targeted for inclusion in
   fix packs 7.0.0.23 and 8.0.0.3.  Please refer to the
   Recommended Updates page for delivery information:
   http://www.ibm.com/support/docview.wss?rs=180&uid=swg27004980 [http://www.ibm.com/support/docview.wss?rs=180&uid=swg27004980]
   
   
    
   
   

TEMPORARY FIX

COMMENTS

results.question: Event Dashboard breaks with Japanese filter Event Dashboard breaks when a filter is created with the browser language set to Japanese. This issue occurs in WebGUI 8.1 Fix Pack 11.
results.ground_truth:
To address the issue of the Event Dashboard breaking when a filter is created with the browser language set to Japanese in WebGUI 8.1 Fix Pack 11, you can follow the steps outlined in the APAR STATUS section of the provided context.

The APAR for this issue is JR46703, which affects BPM ADVANCED on version 8.0.1.0. The problem description states that when users with Non-English locales (e.g. ja_JP) for their browsers access Process Portal, the Dashboards do not display the Pie-Chart and Task lists.

The local fix suggested is to temporarily set the browser language preference to English, access the dashboard, and then change it back to the real locale, such as Japanese. This workaround should help resolve the issue until a permanent fix is implemented.

The problem was addressed by adding a configurable flag that resolves the user's browser locale to a Portal-supported locale and properly sets the language as their User Preference. If the user's browser locale is not a Portal-supported locale, then the user's locale will default to English.

To resolve this issue permanently, you can download the fix package from FixCentral. The fix information including install instructions will be available in the readme.txt file provided with the fix package.

results.answer:
The error "Java was started but returned exit code = -1" when launching IBM Rational Software Architect (RSA) may be due to several factors, including issues with the Java Runtime Environment or video drivers. To resolve this, consider the following steps:
1. Ensure that you have the latest version of the Java Runtime Environment installed.
2. Update your video drivers to the latest version available.
3. Check compatibility between the version of RSA you are using and the Java version.
If the problem persists, further investigation into the specific configuration of your system may be necessary.

Title: TECHQA | Q22 passage 1

RBD; RTC; crashs TECHNOTE (TROUBLESHOOTING)

PROBLEM(ABSTRACT)
 Attempts to use IBM Rational Business Developer connected to an IBM Rational Team Concert server, result in error "Java was started but ... ". 

SYMPTOM
You connected a Rational Team Concert server to Rational Business Developer and you perform actions on the server. Then the Eclipse workbench, java.exe, stops with the following error message. 

Java was started but returned exit code=8096 


[/support/docview.wss?uid=swg21502684&aid=1] [/support/docview.wss?uid=swg21502684&aid=1]

Examples of actions you can do are: creating a work item and creating a user.

CAUSE
One known cause is due to the video driver and when two different versions of drivers are installed on the machine (i.e 6.14.10.5284 and 6.14.10.5303) 


Note: Other causes are also possible but have not been reported.


ENVIRONMENT
This problem has been seen with ThinkPad laptops



DIAGNOSING THE PROBLEM
Look at the java core. The following entries support the hypothesis of the faulty video drivers. 

 

 1. The last call before the problem relates to the Microsoft gdiplus.dll graphics library. 
    
 2. The correct values passes correctly to the Standard Widget Toolkit (SWT) before the error occurs.


RESOLVING THE PROBLEM
Update the video driver to the newest version 

Link: Intel Download Center [http://downloadcenter.intel.com/]

#JVM terminated. Exit code=8096 [https://www.ibm.com/support/docview.wss?uid=swg21303648]
Internet Explorer v9 or higher is required if the produ [http://www-01.ibm.com/support/docview.wss?uid=swg21502701]

PROBLEM(ABSTRACT)
 Attempts to link an IBM Rational Clear Quest (CQ) record to an IBM Rational Team Concert (RTC) work item using RTC Eclipse Client results in the error "Java was started but returned exit code=8096".

Steps to reproduce:

Title: TECHQA | Q22 passage 0

RAD; RSA; Application Developer; Software Architect; debug; debugging; RSA; RAD; debugger; WebSphere Application Server; WAS; terminate; disconnect; 88254 004 000; Java Virtual Machine; behaviour; 1295329 TECHNOTE (FAQ)

QUESTION
 Why does the "Terminate" action provided by WebSphere Application Server debugger not allow you to terminate WebSphere Application Server v6.x when started in debug mode? 

ANSWER
There has been a behavior change in terms of debugging WebSphere Application Server v5.x and v6.x: 

When debugging WebSphere Application Server v5.x you can select any suspended thread, and invoke the "Terminate" action to terminate the server JVM. 

However, when debugging WebSphere Application Server 6.x the "Terminate" action will only terminate or disconnect the WebSphere Application Server debugger. It will not terminate the WebSphere Application Server 6.x JVM process.

results.question: Launching IBM Rational Software Architect application results in Error: Java was started but returned exit code = -1 At times when you launch Rational Software Architect (RSA) application, you might encounter the following error: Error: Java was started but returned exit code = -1
results.ground_truth: This error may indicate a problem with the Java Virtual Machine (JVM) when launching IBM Rational Software Architect. To resolve this issue, you can try updating the video driver to the newest version, as outdated or incompatible drivers can cause JVM termination errors. Another possible cause could be related to a bug in the Intel Graphic Card driver for Windows XP Service Pack 3. If updating the video driver does not solve the problem, you should contact Rational support for further assistance.

results.answer:
To resolve the issue of images not displaying in PDF/Excel outputs in Cognos Analytics 11.0.3, follow these steps:

1. **Check Folder Permissions**: Ensure that the folder containing the images has the correct permissions set. You may need to enable anonymous access in IIS for the Cognos installation and the image folder.

2. **Verify Image Location**: Confirm that the images are located in the specified directory. If they are not, place them in the correct path.

3. **SSL Configuration**: If your environment uses SSL, ensure that the SSL Chain of Trust is correctly configured. You may need to import the necessary CA certificates into the Cognos keystore.

4. **Testing**: After making these changes, run the reports again in PDF/Excel format to check if the images are now displayed correctly.

By following these steps, you should be able to resolve the issue with images not appearing in your PDF/Excel reports.

Title: TECHQA | Q23 passage 3

 TECHNOTE (TROUBLESHOOTING)

PROBLEM(ABSTRACT)
 Unable to view images in reports in PDF/Excel output when using Report Studio/Workspace. 
However images are visible in HTML format when reports are run actively. 

SYMPTOM
Unable to view images in reports in PDF/Excel output when using Report Studio/Workspace. 
However images are visible in HTML format when reports are run actively.

The PDF/Excel outputs will have a placeholder image. This will be in the form of an empty black box (PDF) or will be a missing pictures icon (Excel).


CAUSE
There are a few common causes for this issue: 

 1. The permissions of the folder which contains the image, have not been configured correctly 
 2. The image is not within the directory which is specified 
 3. The directory where the image is meant to be stored, does not exist in the specified location

DIAGNOSING THE PROBLEM
To find out the root cause of this problem, you will need to use tools that monitor and record all actions attempted against the Operating System Registry. 

In THIS scenario the tool used was Process Monitor and the Web Server was IIS 7.5. 

 

 1.  Download Process Monitor tool (Make sure you run it from the same machine Cognos is installed on) 
 2.  Open up the report in Cognos Workspace 
 3.  Load up Process Monitor 
 4.  You will notice the current processes immediately being captured and displaying in the window. Press the Capture / Ctrl+E Icon (Magnifying Glass) to stop the capture of events. 
 5.  Press the Clear / Ctrl+X icon (Eraser) to remove all the recorded entries. 
 6.  Press the capture icon in process monitor 
 7.  Export the report to Excel 2007/PDF 
 8.  Save the file anywhere 
 9.  Press the Capture icon again in process monitor to stop the capturing of events. 
 10. Press the Find Icon / Ctrl+F and type "[Image Name]" to make sure the filter image event was captured. 
 11. You should be able to see the reason why the image could not be saved under the 'Result' column.


RESOLVING THE PROBLEM
If the image is found but cannot be accessed due to permission configuration issue: 

 

 1. Open up IIS 7.5 
 2. Click on the root folder of your Cognos installation (E.g. C1021GA) in the navigation pane on the left side 
 3. When the root folder is selected, double-click 'Authentication' 
 4. Ensure that anonymous access is enabled 
 5. Repeat steps 3 and 4 for the image folder and it's parent folder. 
 6. If the user is concerned about security, they may want to restrict the child-folders (E.g. cgi-bin) and change the authentication settings accordingly 
 7. Run the report in export as Excel 2007 and PDF


If the image/ directory is not located: 

Place the image/directory in the specified location  



Cross reference information Segment Product Component Platform Version Edition Business Analytics Cognos Business Intelligence Cognos Workspace Windows 10.2.1, 10.2

Title: TECHQA | Q23 passage 4

secure socket layer IR440015 TECHNOTE (TROUBLESHOOTING)

PROBLEM(ABSTRACT)
 In a multi-tier environment with SSL enabled on the webserver, images are not being displayed when report run as PDF. Images are being shown as just a blank box in PDF. Running the report in HTML displays the images. 

CAUSE
Incomplete or missing Chain of Trust for SSL within the IBM Cognos Keystore

RESOLVING THE PROBLEM
There are two options available: 

 1. Place the images on the application tier in the Cognos installation directory in the same path they exist on the gateway server. This way the Application Tier can access the image on disk, removing the need to connect to HTTPS
    
    Example: <cognos install>/webcontent/samples/images/pic.jpg 
 2.  
 3. Import the SSL certificate Chain of Trust on the Application Tier.


Steps to import the SSL Chain of Trust: 
 1. Obtain the CA certificate and any Intermediate certificates which is in the chain of the trust that has signed the Web site certificate.
    
 2. Import the certificate(s) in the IBM Cognos CA keystore by typing the following command (from a command window in the <install dir>/bin directory) where ca.cer is the name of the certificate file:

 * ThirdPartyCertificateTool.bat -T -i -r ca.cer -k ../configuration/signkeypair/jCAKeystore -p <keystore_password> 


The default password for the KeyStore is NoPassWordSet. For example: 
 * ThirdPartyCertificateTool.bat -T -i -r ca.cer -k ../configuration/signkeypair/jCAKeystore -p NoPassWordSet 


Note: Ensure that the correct gateway URL with "https" at the beginning has been saved in Cognos Configuration for all dispatchers.

Title: TECHQA | Q23 passage 2

 TECHNOTE(トラブルシューティング)

問題(概要)
 レポートに画像を表示するようにしているが、HTML形式では表示されるのにExcel/PDF形式では表示されない。 

原因
画像のURLで指定されたパスは、ファイルの物理的な場所を参照します。

解決方法
画像のURLは以下のパターンで物理的な場所にマッピングされます。 

使用するURLのパターンに応じて、ファイルをディスパッチャが稼働しているサーバーのフォルダーにも配置します。

   ディスパッチャは以下のフォルダーを参照します。
   
   <c10 install>/webcontent/samples/images/pic.jpg

 *  ./samples/images/pic.jpg ("."で始まる場合) 
 * 
 * 
 * 
 * 
 * 
 * 
 * 
 * 

 * 絶対パスが使用されている場合

/pictures/company_logo.gif、C:\\images\company_logo.gif、\\NAS\ci\company_logo.gif等 

指定した絶対パスが使用されます。 関連情報

Title: TECHQA | Q20 passage 1

Health Dashboard; Event viewer; gauge; web gui TECHNOTE (TROUBLESHOOTING)

PROBLEM(ABSTRACT)
 The event list in the Event Viewer is not updated when clicking on gauges in the Netcool Health Dashboard when using Internet Explorer 9 or 10. When using IE11 or Firefox the problem is not seen. 

SYMPTOM
Step to reproduce: 

1. Log into DASH and open the Health Dashboard
2. Ensure that the event list shown is the Event Viewer, not the AEL
3. Ensure that the active view is the Self Monitoring view
5. Click on a selection of gauges.

Expected result: The event list is updated to show the events which match the related filter (if any such events exist).

Actual result: No update from the Event Viewer. The same events are showing.


CAUSE
This third party API:

var xmlDoc = new ActiveXObject( "Microsoft.XMLDOM" );

in /ibm/console/webtop/scripts/ajax.js causes an exception with some IE9/IE10 browsers.
Stepping over the above line throws the following exception:
eventTransformer: Exception caught while attempting to read payload. Error was:Automation server can't create object

The issue is caused by configuration changes on some Windows machines and is not a defect in OMNIbus Web GUI.


RESOLVING THE PROBLEM
Go to: 

" IE9/10 > Internet Options > Security > Custom Level" and set
1. "Run ActiveX controls and plug-ins" to Enable
2. "Script ActiveX controls marked safe for scripting" to Enable
results.question: Cognos Analytics - Images not displayed in PDF/Excel, working in HTML I've just completed an upgrade to Cognos Analytics 11.0.3. When running existing reports in HTML, the images are displayed but when I try to run them in PDF/Excel they are not.
results.ground_truth:
Based on the provided context, the issue you are experiencing with images not being displayed in PDF/Excel but working in HTML after upgrading to Cognos Analytics 11.0.3 may be due to permissions, file path configurations, or SSL settings. 

To troubleshoot and potentially resolve the issue, you can try the following steps:

1. Check the permissions of the folder containing the images to ensure they are configured correctly.

2. Verify that the images are located within the directory specified in the report.

3. If the image directory is not found, ensure that the images are placed in the specified location.

4. If SSL is enabled and causing issues with image display in PDF, ensure that the SSL certificate Chain of Trust is imported on the Application Tier.

5. Import the CA certificate and any Intermediate certificates in the chain of trust in the IBM Cognos CA keystore using the ThirdPartyCertificateTool.

By following these steps, you may be able to resolve the issue of images not being displayed in PDF/Excel reports in Cognos Analytics 11.0.3.

results.answer:
To configure the machines hosting the WebSphere Operational Decision Management modules for HTTPS and avoid the SSLHandshakeException when executing scenario suites with a Scenario Service Provider (SSP), follow these steps:

1. **Obtain the SSL Certificate**: Ensure you have the SSL certificate for the server you are trying to connect to, including any intermediate certificates in the chain of trust.

2. **Import the Certificate into the Java Keystore**:
   - Use the `keytool` command to import the SSL certificate into the Java keystore. The command will look something like this:
     ```
     keytool -import -alias <alias_name> -file <path_to_certificate_file> -keystore <path_to_keystore> -storepass <keystore_password>
     ```
   - Replace `<alias_name>` with a unique name for the certificate, `<path_to_certificate_file>` with the path to your SSL certificate file, `<path_to_keystore>` with the path to the Java keystore (usually located in the `jre/lib/security/cacerts` directory of your Java installation), and `<keystore_password>` with the keystore password (default is usually `changeit`).

3. **Restart the WebSphere Server**: After importing the certificates, restart the WebSphere server to apply the changes.

4. **Verify Configuration**: Ensure that the HTTPS URL is correctly configured in the Decision Center and that the server is reachable over HTTPS.

By following these steps, you should be able to avoid the SSLHandshakeException and successfully execute scenario suites over HTTPS.

Title: TECHQA | Q24 passage 3

 TECHNOTE (FAQ)

QUESTION
 How do I increase the heap size of the JVM used to run Decision Validation Services (DVS) tests in Rule Studio? 

CAUSE
When running DVS tests from Rule Studio against a local Scenario Service Provider (SSP) service, the environment is subject to the memory limitations of the running Eclipse JVM. 

You may encounter the following error: 

ilog.rules.dvs.util.IlrDVSLogger error
SEVERE: scenario provider or scenario execution threw exception !
adding an error to the report java.lang.OutOfMemoryError: Java heap space


ANSWER
Use Java options, -Xms (initial heap size) and -Xmx (maximum heap size), to increase the heap size of the JVM used to launch the DVS test. 

As you will be changing the default heap configuration of the JVM, we recommend that you designate a separate JRE for the DVS tests to avoid modifying the workspace default JVM options, which may impact other run configurations. 

Follow the steps below: 

 1. In the DVS run configuration, under the JRE tab, select Alternate JRE. Click Installed JREs
    [/support/docview.wss?uid=swg21427125&aid=1] [/support/docview.wss?uid=swg21427125&aid=1] 
    
 2. Select a jdk of your choice and click Duplicate
    [/support/docview.wss?uid=swg21427125&aid=2] [/support/docview.wss?uid=swg21427125&aid=2]
    
    
 3. Set Default VM arguments: use Java options, -Xms and -Xmx, to tune the JVM heap size. Name the new JRE. Click Finish. 
    [/support/docview.wss?uid=swg21427125&aid=3] [/support/docview.wss?uid=swg21427125&aid=3]
    
    
 4. Click OK in the Preferences window. 
 5.  In the Run Configuration window, select the newly added JRE. Click Apply and Run.
    [/support/docview.wss?uid=swg21427125&aid=4] [/support/docview.wss?uid=swg21427125&aid=4]

SUBSCRIBE
You can track all active APARs for this component.



APAR STATUS
 * CLOSED AS PROGRAM ERROR.
    
   
   

ERROR DESCRIPTION
 *  Fail to login to an application with exception:]
   javax.net.ssl.SSLHandshakeException: com.ibm.jsse2.util.j:
   PKIX path building failed:
   java.security.cert.CertPathBuilderException: unable to find
   valid certification path to requested target.
   
   The application relies on the fact that the Liberty profile
   server sets a SSLContext created with the default SSL
   configuration as the process's default.  When running in just
   a Liberty Profile server all is okay.  When they run in a
   collective a cert path error is hit.  This happens because the
   default SSL Configuration is being used when the app make it's
   httpsURLConnection call.
   
   
    
   
   

LOCAL FIX

PROBLEM SUMMARY
 *  ****************************************************************
   * USERS AFFECTED:  All users of IBM WebSphere Application      *
   *                  Server Liberty Profile version 8.5.5        *
   *                  Collective Members.                         *
   ****************************************************************
   * PROBLEM DESCRIPTION: Fail to login to an application with    *
   *                      SSLHandshakeException on a              *
   *                      server with collectiveMember-1.0        *
   *                      feature.                                *
   ****************************************************************
   * RECOMMENDATION:                                              *
   ****************************************************************
   This problem was caused because the code setting the
   SSLSocketFactory for the entire JVM which resulted in
   failure to login to an application with the following
   exception:
   javax.net.ssl.SSLHandshakeException: com.ibm.jsse2.util.j:
   PKIX path building failed:
   java.security.cert.CertPathBuilderException: unable to find
   valid certification path to requested target.
   This can also be caused by application code doing the same
   thing.
   
   
    
   
   

PROBLEM CONCLUSION
 *  We now have instance base setting of the SSL Socket factory on
   the JMXConnector.
   The fix for this APAR is currently targeted for inclusion in
   fix pack 8.5.5.2. Please refer to the Recommended Updates
   page for delivery information:
   http://www.ibm.com/support/docview.wss?rs=180&uid=swg27004980 [http://www.ibm.com/support/docview.wss?rs=180&uid=swg27004980]

Executing a scenario suite with a Scenario Service Provider (SSP) over HTTPS How to configure the machines hosting the WebSphere Operational Decision Management modules with regard to HTTPS when executing scenario suites ?
How to avoid the exception below while requesting from Decision Center the execution of a scenario suite on Scenario Service Provider (SSP) over HTTPS ?
 Exception caught while contacting RES ilog.rules.res.util.http.IlrConnectionException: IO error when contacting "/res/repositoryService"
 ....
 Caused by: javax.net.ssl.SSLHandshakeException: com.ibm.jsse2.util.g: PKIX path building failed: java.security.cert.CertPathBuilderException: unable to find valid certification path to requested target
results.ground_truth:
To avoid the exception while requesting from Decision Center the execution of a scenario suite on Scenario Service Provider (SSP) over HTTPS, you need to ensure that the machines hosting the WebSphere Operational Decision Management modules are properly configured with regard to HTTPS.

Here are the steps to configure the machines:

1. Install the appropriate SSL certificates on all machines involved in the communication process. Make sure that all necessary certificates are added to the truststore of the Decision Center and the Scenario Service Provider.

2. Verify that the SSL configurations are correctly set up on both Decision Center and Scenario Service Provider. Ensure that the SSL configurations match and are compatible with each other.

3. Check the SSL handshake protocol versions used by Decision Center and the SSP. Ensure that both are using a compatible version to establish a secure connection.

4. In the case of PKIX path building failing, make sure that the certificates in the truststore are valid and trusted by all machines involved in the communication.

By following these steps and ensuring proper SSL certificate management and configuration alignment between Decision Center and SSP, you can avoid the SSLHandshakeException related to PKIX path building failure.

results.answer:
To install the IBM Packaging Utility, follow these steps:

1. **Prerequisites**: Ensure that IBM Installation Manager, Version 1.0.0.5, is installed. This is typically done automatically when you attempt to update or install packages using an earlier version.

2. **Download the Fix Pack**: Download the fix pack from the provided URL. Note that the specific version mentioned may no longer be available, so check for the most recent version on the IBM support site.

3. **Extract Files**: Extract the downloaded compressed file to an appropriate directory, such as `C:\temp`.

4. **Add Repository**:
   - Start IBM Installation Manager.
   - On the Start page, click `File > Preferences`, then click `Repositories`.
   - Click `Add Repository` and browse to the extracted directory (e.g., `C:\temp\pu\updates\repository.config`), then click `OK`.
   - Close the Preferences page.

5. **Update or Install**:
   - If IBM Packaging Utility is already installed, click `Update Packages` on the Start page and follow the wizard to update it.
   - If it is not installed, click `Install Packages` on the Start page and follow the wizard to install it.

6. **Follow Installation Instructions**: Complete the installation by following any additional prompts provided by the Installation Manager.

For more detailed instructions, refer to the readme file associated with the version you are installing.

Title: TECHQA | Q25 passage 4

RN1.5; RN1.5.1; RN1.5.2; RN1.5.3; RN1.6 TECHNOTE (TROUBLESHOOTING)

PROBLEM(ABSTRACT)
 You install IBM Packaging Utility on a Linux x86 64-bit computer. You cannot start Packaging Utility. The version of Packaging Utility that you are using requires 32-bit library support when you install with a 64-bit version of IBM Installation Manager. 

SYMPTOM
Different symptoms occur depending on the version of Linux and shells that you use. 

 * No feedback when you start Packaging Utility. 

You start Packaging Utility from a Terminal window running a shell, and change the directory to the installation location of the IBMPU executable. You execute IBMPU. The command returns without providing feedback: 
$ cd /opt/IBM/PackgingUtility
$ ./IBMPU
$ 

 * You receive the message: "no such file or directory"

$ ./IBMPU
bash ./IBMPU no such file or directory
$ 

 * You receive the message: "bad ELF interpreter"

$./IBMPU
./IBMPU bash: ./install: /lib/ld-linux.so.2: bad ELF interpreter: No such file or directory 

 * Check if Packaging Utility is 32-bit

Use the file command to identify a 32-bit executable: 

$ file ./IBMPU
./IBMPU: ELF 32-bit LSB executable, Intel 80386, version 1 (SYSV), dynamically linked (uses shared libs), for GNU/Linux 2.2.5, not stripped 

 * Test the 32-bit Java Runtime Environment (JRE)

The IBMPU executable invokes a program written in Java. Verify that the 32-bit JRE does not run by changing to the jre folder and executing the java command with the -version parameter. Similar results as executing the ./IBMPU are shown: 
$ cd /opt/IBM/PackgingUtility/jre/bin
$ ./java
bash: ./java no such file or directory
$ file ./java
./java: ELF 32-bit LSB executable, Intel 80386, version 1 (SYSV), dynamically linked (uses shared libs), for GNU/Linux 2.2.5, not stripped 

CAUSE
The required 32-bit libraries are not installed on the computer.



RESOLVING THE PROBLEM
To work around this issue, take one of the following actions: 

 

 * Install a new instance of Packaging Utility version 1.6 and select the 64-bit package group.

 * Install the 32-bit libraries that are required to support a 32-bit program


The same techniques for resolving the Installation Manager issues also apply to the Packaging Utility, see Technote #1438776 Issues starting or running Installation Manager [http://www.ibm.com/support/docview.wss?uid=swg21438776]. 
RELATED INFORMATION
#Issues starting or running Installation Manager [http://www-01.ibm.com/support/docview.wss?uid=swg21438776]

Title: TECHQA | Q25 passage 1

RN1.5.2; RN1.5.3 TECHNOTE (TROUBLESHOOTING)

PROBLEM(ABSTRACT)
 You use IBM Packaging Utility to delete a package or fix from a repository. Then, you install a package or a fix from the same repository by using IBM Installation Manager. You receive an error that files cannot be found. 

SYMPTOM
You receive one of the following errors: 

 * From the graphical user interface:

 * Failed to find all installation files.
   
   Message ID: CRIMA1161E
   
   Installation Manager was unable to find all artifacts from input repository. A package has an issue that cannot be resolved by Installation Manager.
   
   Check the package repository for consistency. Otherwise, identify the package that has the issue by looking at the installation history. In Installation Manager, click File > Installation History. In console mode, enter S: View Installation History. Contact IBM customer support.
   
   'jar file <file name>' not found in <repository>.
   
   'zip file <file name>' not found in <repository>.
 * 
 * 
 * From the command line or console mode:

 * CRIMA1154E ERROR: Error installing.
   
   ERROR: 'jar file <file name>' not found in <repository>.
   
   ERROR: 'zip file <file name>' not found in <repository>.



CAUSE
Packaging Utility deletes additional files when you delete a package or fix from a repository. 



RESOLVING THE PROBLEM
This issue has been fixed in Packaging Utility version 1.6.


To work around this issue, use Packaging Utility to recopy the affected package or fix from the original source to the repository that is missing files.

DEFINITIONS
Regression: An error in the Maintenance Delivery Vehicle [http://www.ibm.com/support/docview.wss?uid=swg27008656] (MDV) that produces incorrect or unexpected behavior causing a supported feature to stop functioning as designed. 
This includes: 

 * Coding errors that cause a regression
 * Documentation or packaging problems that cause a regression
 * Errors reported in a new function delivered in a MDV that cause a regression

Incomplete: An error in the MDV has not regressed, but does not work as designed. 
This includes: 

 * Fixed APARs which did not solve the original problem but did not break anything new
 * APARs reporting documentation errors, such as readme errors, that cause problems applying an MDV but do not lead to a regression




Notes:  * Regression and incomplete APARs are considered fix-in-error or MDV-in-error
 * Definitions above apply only to valid APARs that result in product fixes (APARs returned as working-as-designed are not assessed for being fix-in-error)
 * Issues in major releases due to new functionality do not apply in this definition

There are no known regressions to report.

1. Download the fix pack from the provided download URL.
2. Extract the compressed file in an appropriate directory, such as C:\temp.
3. Add the fix pack's repository location in IBM Installation Manager by browsing to the extracted directory.

Title: TECHQA | Q26 passage 0

visibroker; Visibroker; ORB; CORBA; corba; java orb; Java ORB; Orbix; debugging; dumpnameservice; nsdump; dumpns TECHNOTE (FAQ)

QUESTION
 IBM Java ORB : What happened to Visibroker? 

CAUSE
Visibroker was replaced by the IBM Java ORB in 2012

ANSWER
 

The Visibroker CORBA software is being phased out of the integrations product line. New probes will use the ORB provided with the IBM JRE included within the base installation of Netcool/OMNIbus. Ensure the probes readme or description files are read before installation to ensure that the necessary dependencies are installed first. 

 

The dumpnameservice.sh utility uses Visibroker [not Visibroker8] and Visibroker needs to be installed to use it. 

Its replacement is dumpns, as provided with the latest CORBA framework package [probe-corba-framework 3_0 or above]. The command is located in the $NCHOME/omnibus/probes/java/corba directory and takes the host port as arguments. 

You can also use the standard jacorb.jar to dump the name service contents using this syntax:
java -cp /tmp/jacorb.jar org.jacorb.naming.ContextLister -url corbaloc:iiop:<naming-server-ip>:<naming-server-port>/NameService

Listing output:
TMF_MTNM.Class/
TejasNetworks.Vendor/
TejasNetworks\/NORTH-CDG.EmsInstance/
3\.5.Version/
TejasNetworks\/NORTH-CDG.EmsSessionFactory_I

Example probe property:
NamingContextPath: "TMF_MTNM.Class/TejasNetworks.Vendor/TejasNetworks\\/NORTH-CDG.EmsInstance/3\\.5.Version/TejasNetworks\\/NORTH-CDG.EmsSessionFactory_I"

Note : jacorb.jar is available from the JACORB JAR download link. 

 

Debugging the Java ORB: 

The latest CORBA framework allows probes to use the ORB debugging properties: 

e.g.
ORBDebug : 'true'
ORBDebugFile : '/tmp/orb_debug.log'

Use the -dumpprops option on the probe to see if these properties are available or not.


To manually debug the IBM Java ORB the probe specific environment file needs to be modified as described below: 


Edit or create the $OMNIHOME/probes/java/nco_p_<probe>.env file and add the following line to enable IBM ORB trace logging.

NCO_JPROBE_JAVA_FLAGS="-Dcom.ibm.CORBA.Debug=true -Dcom.ibm.CORBA.CommTrace=true -Dcom.ibm.CORBA.Debug.Output=$OMNIHOME/log/ibm_orb_trace.log $NCO_JPROBE_JAVA_FLAGS"

Notes: 
The ORB debugging should not be enabled unless required and not left running in a production environment unless requested by IBM Tivoli Netcool support.

If the property ORB debugging is available in the probe, the manual java option method does not work.

If the probe reports encoding issues when used with the IBM Java ORB set the probe properties as shown below before contacting support;
ORBWCharDefault : "UTF16"
ORBCharEncoding : "ISO8859_1"


RELATED INFORMATION
#JACORB JAR download [http://www.jacorb.org/download.html]

Title: TECHQA | Q6 passage 0

SQL integration; ORA-01000; statement cache; bind variable; SQL TECHNOTE (TROUBLESHOOTING)

PROBLEM(ABSTRACT)
 You can use IBM Business Process Manager SQL integration service to interact with the database. In a high workload, you might find that the prepared statement cache hit ratio is very low for the data source that is used by the SQL integration service. After increasing the statement cache size in the data source, an Oracle ORA-01000 error happens. 

SYMPTOM
You might see the following exception in SystemOut.log: 

java.sql.SQLException: ORA-01000: maximum open cursors exceeded
at oracle.jdbc.driver.T4CTTIoer.processError(T4CTTIoer.java:439)
at oracle.jdbc.driver.T4CTTIoer.processError(T4CTTIoer.java:395)
at oracle.jdbc.driver.T4C8Oall.processError(T4C8Oall.java:802)
at oracle.jdbc.driver.T4CTTIfun.receive(T4CTTIfun.java:436)
at oracle.jdbc.driver.T4CTTIfun.doRPC(T4CTTIfun.java:186)
at oracle.jdbc.driver.T4C8Oall.doOALL(T4C8Oall.java:521)
at oracle.jdbc.driver.T4CPreparedStatement.doOall8(T4CPreparedStatement.java:205)
at oracle.jdbc.driver.T4CPreparedStatement.executeForRows(T4CPreparedStatement.java:1008)
at oracle.jdbc.driver.OracleStatement.doExecuteWithTimeout(OracleStatement.java:1307)
at oracle.jdbc.driver.OraclePreparedStatement.executeInternal(OraclePreparedStatement.java:3449)
at oracle.jdbc.driver.OraclePreparedStatement.execute(OraclePreparedStatement.java:3550)
at oracle.jdbc.driver.OraclePreparedStatementWrapper.execute(OraclePreparedStatementWrapper.java:1374)
at com.ibm.ws.rsadapter.jdbc.WSJdbcPreparedStatement.pmiExecute(WSJdbcPreparedStatement.java:958)
at com.ibm.ws.rsadapter.jdbc.WSJdbcPreparedStatement.execute(WSJdbcPreparedStatement.java:625)
at teamworks.sql.SQLClient.executePreparedStatement(SQLClient.java:443)
at teamworks.sql.SQLClient.execute(SQLClient.java:216)
at teamworks.SQLConnector$5.execute(SQLConnector.java:232)
at teamworks.SQLConnector$5.execute(SQLConnector.java:228)
at teamworks.sql.SQLExecutor.doExecute(SQLExecutor.java:134)
at teamworks.sql.SQLExecutor.execute(SQLExecutor.java:79)
at teamworks.SQLConnector.execute(SQLConnector.java:227)


CAUSE
The Oracle ORA-01000 error occurs because the statement cache size property for the data source is set to a value higher than the maximum number of open cursors in the Oracle database. But, in this case, it is not good to simply increase the maximum number of open cursors in the Oracle database. 

The root cause is that bind variable is not used in your SQL integration service. The SQL string that is passed to the SQL integration service contains literal values. Because most of the SQL statements for one SQL integration service have different literal values, a matching statement cannot be found in statement cache (shared pool). The cache hit ratio is very low and the execution plan is generated for each SQL statement every time.


RESOLVING THE PROBLEM
To resolve this issue, use a bind variable in your SQL integration service. 

For information on how to use the bind variable in the IBM Business Process Manager SQL integration service, see the following developerWorks article: Using the SQL integration service with WebSphere Lombardi Edition V7.2 and WebSphere Application Server V7 [http://www.ibm.com/developerworks/websphere/tutorials/1110_phi/]. 



To learn more about the Oracle ORA-01000 error and its corresponding solution, see the "ORA-01000: maximum open cursors exceeded" connecting to an Oracle database from WebSphere Application Server [http://www.ibm.com/support/docview.wss?uid=swg21218727] document.

 


Cross reference information Segment Product Component Platform Version Edition Business Integration IBM Business Process Manager Standard Databases AIX, Linux, Linux zSeries, Solaris, Windows 8.5, 8.0.1, 8.0, 7.5.1, 7.5 Business Integration IBM Business Process Manager Express Databases Linux, Windows 8.5, 8.0.1, 8.0, 7.5.1, 7.5 
PRODUCT ALIAS/SYNONYM
 BPM

results.question: What versions of WSAS using Oracle 12c are supported? What versions of WSAS using Oracle 12c are supported?
results.ground_truth:
The versions of WebSphere Application Server (WSAS) using Oracle 12c that are supported are as follows:

- For IBM Emptoris Contract Management 9.5 through 10.0.2.5, WSAS version 8.0.0.x with Interim Fix PI27100 is supported.
- For IBM Emptoris Program Management 10.0.0 through 10.0.2.5, WSAS version 8.5.0.x with Interim Fix PI27099 is supported.
- For IBM Emptoris Sourcing 10.0.0 through 10.0.2.5, WSAS version 8.5.0.x with Interim Fix PI27099 is supported.
- For IBM Emptoris Spend Analysis 10.0.0 through 10.0.2.5, WSAS version 8.5.0.x with Interim Fix PI27099 is supported.
- For IBM Emptoris Supplier Lifecycle Management 9.5 through 10.0.2.5, WSAS version 8.5.0.x with Interim Fix PI27099 is supported.
- For IBM Emptoris Strategic Supply Management 10.0.0 through 10.0.2.5, WSAS version 8.5.5.x with Interim Fix PI27099 is supported.
- For IBM Emptoris Rivermine Telecom Expense Management 6.5.x through 7.0.1, WSAS version 8.5.5.x with Interim Fix PI27099 is supported.
- For IBM Emptoris Services Procurement 10.0.0, WSAS version 8.5.5.x with Interim Fix PI27099 is supported.

These versions of WSAS with the specified Interim Fixes are compatible with Oracle 12c when running IBM Emptoris applications.

results.answer:
Yes, there are known issues related to TEP to TEPS communication with different Java versions, particularly with Java 8. Some of the key issues include:

1. **Java 8 with Internet Explorer 11**: Users may encounter a message stating, "This content cannot be displayed in a frame." The workaround is to open the content in a new window or use Java 8 32-bit instead of 64-bit.

2. **Java 8 with Firefox**: The TEP client may fail to fully initialize, resulting in a blank screen. The recommended workaround is to use Internet Explorer or the WebStart client.

3. **Connection Problems**: Recent Java updates have caused various connection issues when logging into the portal server via the TEP client. It is advised to refer to technote #1672459 for the latest Java support requirements.

For a comprehensive list of known issues and fixes, it is recommended to check the IBM support documentation and the specific technotes mentioned.
contexts:
Title: TECHQA | Q28 passage 1

tep client; java web start; Unable to load resource; java.net.ConnectException; Connection refused: connect; 15200; windows 7; jnlp; jre TECHNOTE (TROUBLESHOOTING)

PROBLEM(ABSTRACT)
 After upgrading to Windows 7, TEP client (Java WebStart method) no longer starts. 

SYMPTOM
The execution fails with the following errors :

com.sun.deploy.net.FailedDownloadException: Unable to load resource: 

 http://host.xxx.dev.yyy:15200/commons-discovery-0.2.jar.jnlp 


at com.sun.deploy.net.DownloadEngine.actionDownload(Unknown Source)
at com.sun.deploy.net.DownloadEngine.getCacheEntry(Unknown Source)
at com.sun.deploy.net.DownloadEngine.getCacheEntry(Unknown Source)
..
..
..
Wrapped Exception

java.net.ConnectException: Connection refused: connect
at java.net.PlainSocketImpl.socketConnect(Native Method)
at java.net.PlainSocketImpl.doConnect(Unknown Source)
at java.net.PlainSocketImpl.connectToAddress(Unknown Source)
at java.net.PlainSocketImpl.connect(Unknown Source)



CAUSE
Possible explanations for this failure are :

1) IHS not running on TEPS system
2) Firewall preventing communication with port 15200
3) Corrupted or invalid local files on workstation machine where TEP client is launched

Nothing explicitly related with Windows 7, unless specific firewall rules have been applied with the new
operating system


RESOLVING THE PROBLEM
1) Check if port 15200 is in LISTENING on the TEPS server.If not, likely the IHS is not running and you need to investigate about failures on TEPS side, considering that IHS should be started and stopped automatically with TEPS service.

2) If port 15200 is available and in LISTENING on TEPS, verify if any firewall (locally to the workstation that is trying to launch TEP or the network boundary firewall) is preventing communication with that specific port. You may need to change firewall rules to allow access on port 15200.

If the first two options are fine, communication works normally so it is possible that the problem is with the application or JNLP file locally stored on the Windows workstation.

3) On the workstation where TEP client runs, from Windows Control Panel, open the Java Control Panel and clear the Java cache.Then download TEP application again using the following URL from a web browser: 

 http://TEPS_host_name:1920///cnp/kdh/lib/tep.jnlp 

In this way you will get rid of any inconsistency of the TEP client application previously downloaded.



 

PRODUCT ALIAS/SYNONYM
 IBM Tivoli Monitoring V6.2.3 Tivoli Enterprise Portal Client

You connected a Rational Team Concert server to Rational Business Developer and you perform actions on the server. Then the Eclipse workbench, java.exe, stops with the following error message.

results.question: TEP to TEPS communication issues with different Java versions Recent Java updates have caused connection problems when logging into the TEPS via the TEP client (Browser or Java Web Start). Is there an updated list of known issues?
results.ground_truth:
Yes, there are known issues with TEP to TEPS communication when using different Java versions. Some of the known issues include:
1) Using Java 8 64-bit with Internet Explorer version 11 fails with a message stating "This content cannot be displayed in a frame."
- Workaround: Use Java 8 32-bit with Internet Explorer 11 or use the WebStart client.
2) Java 8 fails with Firefox, and the client does not fully initialize, giving a blank screen.
- Workaround: Use Internet Explorer or the WebStart client. This issue will be fixed in Oracle Java 8 u60, targeted for August 2015.

For more information on these known issues and the latest Java Support requirements, you can refer to technote #1672459:
http://www.ibm.com/support/docview.wss?uid=swg21672459

results.answer:
Configuring the J2EE Tomcat Data Collector (DC) to run as a Windows Service is not explicitly covered in the provided context. However, this type of configuration is generally supported. You can use tools like Apache Commons Daemon (procrun) to set up Tomcat as a Windows Service. Here are the general steps you might follow:

1. **Download and Install Procrun**: Obtain the Procrun utility from the Apache Commons Daemon project.

2. **Create a Service**: Use the Procrun command line to create a new service for your Tomcat instance. You will need to specify the executable path, service name, and other parameters.

3. **Configure the Service**: Ensure that the service is configured with the correct Java Runtime Environment (JRE) and that the necessary environment variables (like `JAVA_HOME`) are set.

4. **Set Permissions**: Make sure that the user account running the service has the necessary permissions to access the Tomcat directories and logs.

5. **Start the Service**: Once configured, you can start the service through the Windows Services management console or using the command line.

For detailed instructions, please refer to the official ITCAM for J2EE documentation or relevant support resources, as they may provide specific guidance tailored to your version and setup.

QUESTION
 Downloading and installing the Tomcat Apache server in preparation for ITCAM monitoring. 

ANSWER
So you want to download, install and monitor the Apache Tomcat server on your Windows system but don't quite know where to start? 

Well, start here...

A. How to download the Apache Tomcat Server:
v5.5 is here: https://archive.apache.org/dist/tomcat/tomcat-5/v5.5.26/bin/ [https://archive.apache.org/dist/tomcat/tomcat-5/v5.5.26/bin/]
v6.0, v7.0, v8.0 is here: http://tomcat.apache.org/whichversion.html [http://tomcat.apache.org/whichversion.html]- 
- click on the version on the left and download the zip file to your C:\temp directory
(Note: We don't support the actual Apache Tomcat server itself. For help with that, consult one of the many online forums.)

B. How to install the Apache Tomcat Server:
Installing is super easy, there's no GUI to run, there's no silent install, nothing.. Simply extract the zip file to your C:\Program Files(x86)\ dir. (Or any dir you want.) See my example here.. extracted to C:\Program Files(x86) directory. You will find a sub-directory under this directory called apache-tomcat-5.5.26 (or whatever version you install.)

[/support/docview.wss?uid=swg21682163&aid=1] [/support/docview.wss?uid=swg21682163&aid=1]

C. How to stop / start up the Apache Tomcat Server:
In this example, am using Apache Tomcat v7.0.55. 
Set the JRE_HOME and check your java version. Then run the startup.bat script which will start the Tomcat Server.
[/support/docview.wss?uid=swg21682163&aid=2] [/support/docview.wss?uid=swg21682163&aid=2]
(To stop the Apache Tomcat Server, simply run the shutdown.bat script from the same bin dir.)

D. How to login to the Apache Tomcat server admin console:
Bring up your browser, and simply point to your server on port 8080 (default) like this:
http://<hostname_or_IP>:8080

E. How to monitor Apache Tomcat server:
- download and install the latest version of ITCAM for J2EE v7.1.1.x
- configured the ITCAM agent and DC
- restart your Server, login to your TEPS and observe data

Title: TECHQA | Q28 passage 1

tep client; java web start; Unable to load resource; java.net.ConnectException; Connection refused: connect; 15200; windows 7; jnlp; jre TECHNOTE (TROUBLESHOOTING)

PROBLEM(ABSTRACT)
 After upgrading to Windows 7, TEP client (Java WebStart method) no longer starts. 

SYMPTOM
The execution fails with the following errors :

com.sun.deploy.net.FailedDownloadException: Unable to load resource: 

 http://host.xxx.dev.yyy:15200/commons-discovery-0.2.jar.jnlp 


at com.sun.deploy.net.DownloadEngine.actionDownload(Unknown Source)
at com.sun.deploy.net.DownloadEngine.getCacheEntry(Unknown Source)
at com.sun.deploy.net.DownloadEngine.getCacheEntry(Unknown Source)
..
..
..
Wrapped Exception

java.net.ConnectException: Connection refused: connect
at java.net.PlainSocketImpl.socketConnect(Native Method)
at java.net.PlainSocketImpl.doConnect(Unknown Source)
at java.net.PlainSocketImpl.connectToAddress(Unknown Source)
at java.net.PlainSocketImpl.connect(Unknown Source)



CAUSE
Possible explanations for this failure are :

1) IHS not running on TEPS system
2) Firewall preventing communication with port 15200
3) Corrupted or invalid local files on workstation machine where TEP client is launched

Nothing explicitly related with Windows 7, unless specific firewall rules have been applied with the new
operating system


RESOLVING THE PROBLEM
1) Check if port 15200 is in LISTENING on the TEPS server.If not, likely the IHS is not running and you need to investigate about failures on TEPS side, considering that IHS should be started and stopped automatically with TEPS service.

2) If port 15200 is available and in LISTENING on TEPS, verify if any firewall (locally to the workstation that is trying to launch TEP or the network boundary firewall) is preventing communication with that specific port. You may need to change firewall rules to allow access on port 15200.

If the first two options are fine, communication works normally so it is possible that the problem is with the application or JNLP file locally stored on the Windows workstation.

3) On the workstation where TEP client runs, from Windows Control Panel, open the Java Control Panel and clear the Java cache.Then download TEP application again using the following URL from a web browser: 

 http://TEPS_host_name:1920///cnp/kdh/lib/tep.jnlp 

In this way you will get rid of any inconsistency of the TEP client application previously downloaded.

Title: TECHQA | Q29 passage 3

Data Collector; NetWeaver; configuration; permissions; group; access TECHNOTE (TROUBLESHOOTING)

PROBLEM(ABSTRACT)
 When multiple instances of SAP NetWeaver are installed on a single operating system instance, configuration of the Data Collector for one or several NetWeaver instances fails. 

CAUSE
You need to set permissions for the Data Collector installation directory.

DIAGNOSING THE PROBLEM
The following messages might be present in the configuration log file: 

User that runs NetWeaver application server can not access the ITCAM DC home directory, or the user is not the member of the user group of ITCAM DC.


RESOLVING THE PROBLEM
The admin users for every SAP NetWeaver instance must belong to the same group (for example, sapsys). 

When installing the Data Collector, run the installation program as a user belonging to the same group. Alternatively, change the group of the Data Collector installation tree (DC_home) to this group, for example: 

chgrp -R sapsys /opt/IBM/itcam/J2EE/DC 

For all files and directories in the tree, the group must have at least the same permissions as the owner. To achieve this in the simplest way, grant the group read, write, and execute permissions for all files in the tree, for example: 

chown -R g+rwx /opt/IBM/itcam/J2EE/DC 

Also, make sure that the group has read and write permissions for the Tivoli logging directory (by default, /var/ibm/tivoli/common), 

To configure each SAP NetWeaver instance that you need to monitor, run the Data Collector configuration tool using the admin user for the instance.
